name: update_change_2_non_queue_rid_droppable
code:
proof fn phi_1_update_change_2_non_queue_rid_droppable<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    rid: ReqId,
    logLower: LogIdx,
    logUpper: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        logLower <= logUpper,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates),
        updates.contains_key(rid),
        !queue.contains(rid),
    ensures
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates.remove(rid)),
{
    LogRangeMatchesQueue_update_change_2(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates, updates.remove(rid));
}
reason: Any update entry not in the queue can be silently removed — the frame condition only guards queue-contained rids, allowing arbitrary mutation of non-queue entries

name: update_change_2_empty_updates_all_remote
code:
proof fn phi_2_update_change_2_empty_updates_all_remote<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    logLower: LogIdx,
    logUpper: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        logLower <= logUpper,
        queue.len() == 0,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, Map::empty()),
{
    LogRangeMatchesQueue_update_change_2(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates, Map::empty());
}
reason: When the queue is empty (all remote entries), the updates map can be replaced with an entirely empty map — shows the match is completely independent of updates for all-remote ranges

name: update_change_2_swap_non_queue_state
code:
proof fn phi_3_update_change_2_swap_non_queue_state<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    rid: ReqId,
    new_state: UpdateState<DT>,
    logLower: LogIdx,
    logUpper: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        logLower <= logUpper,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates),
        updates.contains_key(rid),
        !queue.contains(rid),
    ensures
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates.insert(rid, new_state)),
{
    LogRangeMatchesQueue_update_change_2(queue, log, 0, logLower, logUpper, nodeId, updates, updates.insert(rid, new_state));
}
reason: A non-queue rid's update state can be changed to any arbitrary state (Init, Applied, Done) without affecting the match — the frame condition is blind to non-queue entries

name: local_entry_queue_rid_in_updates
code:
proof fn phi_4_local_entry_queue_rid_in_updates<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    logIdx: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logIdx + 1),
        log[logIdx].node_id == nodeId,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logIdx, logIdx + 1, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        queue.len() == 1,
        updates.contains_key(queue[0]),
        updates[queue[0]] is Placed,
        updates[queue[0]].arrow_Placed_idx() == logIdx,
{
}
reason: Single local log entry forces exactly one queue element in Placed state linking to that log index

name: empty_log_range_exhausts_queue
code:
proof fn phi_5_empty_log_range_exhausts_queue<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    idx: LogIdx,
    qi: nat,
)
    requires
        qi <= queue.len(),
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, qi, idx, idx, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        qi == queue.len(),
{
}
reason: Empty log range forces queue index to equal queue length — base case of the recursion
